explanationZhTw: 門把；這裡是一般實體名詞，指門上的把手。
generatedExample: She turned the door-knob slowly and stepped inside.

explanationZhTw: 日記本；指用來寫下私人記錄或想法的本子。
generatedExample: He wrote about his day in his diary before bed.

explanationZhTw: 弄髒、弄花；這裡指把墨水或表面弄出髒痕。
generatedExample: Try not to smudge the paint while it is still fresh.

explanationZhTw: 奶油色的、偏米白的；這裡是在描述紙張顏色，不是「奶油味」的意思。
generatedExample: The notebook had creamy pages that looked old and soft.

explanationZhTw: 表示「吸了一口氣」；這裡用來描寫他因緊張或準備而先深吸氣，再去開門。
generatedExample: She drew in her breath before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，意思是「一陣強烈的如釋重負感湧上心頭」；不是字面上的水流，而是情緒突然放鬆。
generatedExample: A wave of relief flowed through the team when the results were announced.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「沒有顏色」，而是形容這位女人看起來蒼白、沒有精神，帶有疲憊和黯淡的感覺。
generatedExample: After the long journey, he looked colourless and tired.

explanationZhTw: 形容外表像是被壓垮、受挫或精疲力盡；屬於帶有情緒和狀態意味的描寫，不是字面上的「被壓碎」。
generatedExample: He came home looking crushed after hearing the bad news.

explanationZhTw: wispy 形容「細軟稀疏、飄散的」；這裡是在描寫頭髮又細又少、看起來不濃密。
generatedExample: The child had wispy hair that fell across his forehead.

explanationZhTw: 指臉上有很多皺紋或紋路，通常表示年紀較大或經歷勞累。
generatedExample: The old man had a lined face and kind eyes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `comrade` 是稱呼語，不是單純的「同志／同伴」字面意思；在這類政治語境中，常用來直接稱呼對方，帶有特定時代色彩。
generatedExample: Comrade, could you help me with this form?

explanationZhTw: `dreary` 表示「沉悶的、令人沮喪的、無趣的」，用來形容聲音時，帶出低落而單調的感覺。
generatedExample: The hotel room felt dreary after the long rain.

explanationZhTw: `whining` 形容聲音「尖聲抱怨、嘮叨」，帶有惹人煩的語氣，不只是單純的發牢騷。
generatedExample: He answered in a whining voice that made everyone impatient.

explanationZhTw: `come in` 這裡是片語動詞，意思是「進來、進到室內」，屬於很常見的基礎用法。
generatedExample: Please come in and take a seat.

explanationZhTw: `come across` 在這裡不是「偶然碰到」，而是口語中的「過來、走過來」；要看上下文才能知道是動作方向。
generatedExample: Could you come across after work and help me?

explanationZhTw: `have a look` 是常見口語說法，意思是「看一下、檢查一下」。
generatedExample: Have a look at this message and tell me what you think.

explanationZhTw: `blocked up` 這裡表示「堵住了、塞住了」，特別常用來說排水管、管道或孔洞被堵塞。
generatedExample: The drain got blocked up again last night.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「不鼓勵、視為不恰當」；語氣偏書面，也比較少見。
generatedExample: The school discountenanced the use of phones during class.

explanationZhTw: 指「非專業的、業餘的」；這裡強調修理工作不是由正式技工處理。
generatedExample: He made an amateur attempt at fixing the sink.

explanationZhTw: 指「惱人的事、煩擾」；這裡不是醫學上的刺激，而是日常令人不舒服的麻煩。
generatedExample: The constant noise became a real irritation.

explanationZhTw: 表示「左右、大約」；用來說明年份只是估計值。
generatedExample: The house was built in 1980 or thereabouts.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「狀況很差、快要散架」；可用來形容建築、物品或制度。
generatedExample: The old bicycle is falling to pieces.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「半個蒸汽」，而是表示「半功率、沒有全力運作」。
generatedExample: The factory was running at half steam after the power cut.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「關閉、停用」；這裡指暖氣系統被完全關掉。
generatedExample: The cafe was closed down for repairs.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「經核准、被批准」；語氣正式，也帶有官僚意味。
generatedExample: The project must be sanctioned by the manager first.

explanationZhTw: 表示「耽擱、拖延」；這裡指連修理窗玻璃都可能被拖很久。
generatedExample: Traffic will hold up the delivery if we leave late.

explanationZhTw: 「Of course」在這裡表示「當然、難怪」，用來指出前面的話似乎很明顯或合情合理。
generatedExample: Of course she was late; the train had been delayed for an hour.

explanationZhTw: 「vaguely」表示「含糊地、模模糊糊地」，說話或表達不清楚、不確定。
generatedExample: He vaguely remembered meeting her at a conference last year.

explanationZhTw: 灰暗、髒舊的；不是亮堂或乾淨的樣子。
generatedExample: The hallway looked dingy after years of smoke and dust.

explanationZhTw: 破損不堪的；表面看起來被用壞、撞壞了。
generatedExample: His battered suitcase still worked, but barely.

explanationZhTw: 被踩踏過的；在這裡形容東西看起來又髒又被弄得很亂。
generatedExample: The grass looked trampled-on after the concert.

explanationZhTw: （正式、少見）雜七雜八的裝備；這裡指一堆運動器材。
generatedExample: We had to carry all our impedimenta up the stairs.

explanationZhTw: 書頁邊角磨損、翻卷的；常用來形容很舊、常被翻看的書。
generatedExample: I found a dog-eared map in the glove box.

explanationZhTw: 為……所共有；這裡表示整棟樓都聞得到同樣的味道。
generatedExample: A salty smell common to seaside towns drifted through the market.

explanationZhTw: 夾雜著、摻雜著；表示原本的氣味中又混入另一種更強烈的味道。
generatedExample: Her speech was shot through by moments of anger and regret.

explanationZhTw: 強烈難聞的氣味；通常帶有明顯的貶義。
generatedExample: The alley reeked of stale beer and garbage.

explanationZhTw: 一聞就知道；指只靠第一次聞到味道就立刻察覺。
generatedExample: At the first sniff, she knew the soup had gone bad.

explanationZhTw: 跟著節拍唱或配合旋律；這裡是勉強跟著音樂哼唱的意思。
generatedExample: He tried to keep tune with the song, but he was out of key.

explanationZhTw: 從……傳出、發出；這裡指音樂正從螢幕裡播出。
generatedExample: Warm air was issuing from the cracked vent.

explanationZhTw: 表示「有點擔心、半帶戒備」；`half-` 在這裡不是字面上的「一半」，而是用來弱化語氣，表示程度不高。
generatedExample: She gave him a half-apprehensive look before opening the door.

explanationZhTw: `breaking off` 在這裡是「突然中斷、停住不說」的意思，指她說話常常講到一半就停下來。
generatedExample: He kept breaking off in the middle of his explanation.

explanationZhTw: `to the brim` 是慣用說法，意思是「裝到滿得快溢出來」；這裡形容水槽幾乎滿到頂了。
generatedExample: The cup was filled to the brim with hot tea.

explanationZhTw: `filthy` 是「骯髒的、污穢的」；這裡用來強調水又髒又噁心。
generatedExample: My hands were filthy after fixing the bike.

explanationZhTw: `liable to` 在這裡不是「有責任」，而是「容易、很可能會」的意思；意思是彎下去很容易讓他開始咳嗽。
generatedExample: This old machine is liable to break down in cold weather.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「把它修好／處理好」。在口語裡常用來表示修正問題或讓事情恢復正常，不是字面上的「放對位置」。
generatedExample: Don't worry, Tom will put it right by tomorrow.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「立刻、馬上、很快就會」。在這種語境中是時間上的迅速反應，不是指很長的一段時間。
generatedExample: If you call her, she'll reply in a moment.

explanationZhTw: 這是英式口語強調語氣，意思是「非常、很」。它比一般的 `very` 更有說話感，常見於較口語或較老式的表達。
generatedExample: She's ever so kind to everyone she meets.

explanationZhTw: 同事；指在同一機構或單位一起工作的人。
generatedExample: She asked her fellow-employee for help with the report.

explanationZhTw: 被迫趕走、被迫離開；這裡不是指搬家，而是指被組織除名或趕出。
generatedExample: After the scandal, several members were unwillingly evicted from the committee.

explanationZhTw: 繼續留下來；超過原本該離開的時間還待著。
generatedExample: I stayed on after the meeting to finish a few tasks.

explanationZhTw: 法定年齡；由法律或規定明訂的年齡限制。
generatedExample: You cannot apply for the role until you reach the statutory age.

explanationZhTw: 下級職位、較低階的工作。
generatedExample: She spent two years in a subordinate post before getting promoted.

explanationZhTw: 露個面、短暫出席一下；重點是有出現，但不一定久留。
generatedExample: He put in an appearance at the party, then left after ten minutes.

explanationZhTw: 跟著他到處走；這裡是說汗味一路伴隨著他。
generatedExample: The assistant followed him about the store while he was shopping.

explanationZhTw: `spanner` 指「扳手」，是英式英文常用的工具名稱；在這裡不是比喻用法。
generatedExample: Can you pass me the spanner so I can tighten this bolt?

explanationZhTw: `fiddling with` 表示「隨手擺弄、反覆調整」；這裡帶有不太認真、邊弄邊試的感覺。
generatedExample: She was fiddling with the radio until the signal came back.

explanationZhTw: `nut` 在這裡是「螺帽」，屬於機械零件的用法，不是常見的「堅果」意思。
generatedExample: The nut came loose, so I had to tighten it again.

explanationZhTw: `angle-joint` 是技術性名詞，指「角接頭／彎角接合處」一類的零件或連接部位；屬於專門用語。
generatedExample: He checked the angle-joint before turning the water back on.

explanationZhTw: 英式英文中指「扳手」或「板手」，是用來轉動螺帽、螺栓的工具。
generatedExample: He grabbed a spanner and tightened the bolt.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是生物學上的「無脊椎動物」，而是比喻她突然變得軟弱、沒主見，好像一下子失去骨氣。
generatedExample: When the manager challenged him, he became invertebrate and stopped arguing.

explanationZhTw: 「charged into」表示「衝進、猛衝進去」，帶有很快、很突然的動作感。
generatedExample: The players charged into the field when the match began.

explanationZhTw: 「living-room」是「客廳」，屬於日常居家空間的常見說法。
generatedExample: We put the sofa in the living-room.

explanationZhTw: 「spanner」是「扳手」，指用來轉動螺帽或修理管件的工具。
generatedExample: He used a spanner to tighten the bolt.

explanationZhTw: 「let out」在這裡是「放出、排出」的意思，不是「讓出去」。
generatedExample: She let out the air from the balloon.

explanationZhTw: 「clot」原本常指「凝塊、血塊」，這裡是較特殊的用法，指一團糾結成塊的頭髮或髒東西。
generatedExample: A clot of mud stuck to his boot.

explanationZhTw: 「blocked up」表示「堵住、塞住」，這裡指頭髮把管子堵塞了。
generatedExample: The sink was blocked up with food scraps.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `savage` 不是「野蠻人」的字面意思，而是形容聲音兇狠、粗暴、充滿威嚇感。
generatedExample: A savage voice came from the alley, and everyone froze.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，意思是「突然冒出來；一下子出現」。這裡指男孩從桌子後面突然站/跳出來。
generatedExample: A cat popped up from behind the sofa.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「帶威脅地逼近；恐嚇」的意思，不只是普通的「討厭」，而是讓人感到有危險。
generatedExample: The dog was menacing the strangers at the gate.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「邪惡」，而是「兇狠的；帶有攻擊性的」。
generatedExample: He gave me a vicious look when I asked the question.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，意思是「舉止；神情；態度」。
generatedExample: Her calm demeanour helped everyone stay relaxed.

explanationZhTw: traitor 指「叛徒」；這裡是非常強烈的指控，意思是背叛自己人或自己的陣營。
generatedExample: After the leak, he was treated like a traitor by both sides.

explanationZhTw: thought-criminal 在《一九八四》裡是「思想犯」；不是因為做了什麼，而是連想法都被政權視為罪行。
generatedExample: In the novel, even a private doubt can make you a thought-criminal.

explanationZhTw: Eurasian spy 指「歐亞國的間諜」；在這裡是政治宣傳式的指控，帶有敵我分類的語氣。
generatedExample: The newspaper claimed he was a Eurasian spy, but no evidence was ever shown.

explanationZhTw: vaporize 在這裡不是字面上的「蒸發」，而是極權語境下的「抹除／處決」；是刻意帶有冷酷官腔的用法。
generatedExample: The regime threatened to vaporize anyone who spoke out.

explanationZhTw: salt mines 在這裡不是單純的地名，而是「去採鹽礦服苦役」的威脅，表示重刑勞役。
generatedExample: He joked that late homework would send us to the salt mines.

explanationZhTw: 在《1984》的語境裡，這是指「思想犯」：不是一般的刑事罪，而是連想法都可能被政權視為犯罪。
generatedExample: In the novel, even a private doubt can make someone a thought-criminal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是普通的「跳躍」，而是指動物幼崽輕快地嬉跳、翻滾；帶有一種天真卻又暗藏危險的感覺。
generatedExample: The lambs were gambolling across the field in the afternoon sun.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 calculating 不是「計算」，而是「精心盤算、冷酷算計的」；整個片語形容一種有預謀的兇狠。
generatedExample: Her calculating ferocity made everyone in the room back away.

explanationZhTw: 這是英式口語，意思是「幸好、真是萬幸」。
generatedExample: It was a good job we left early, or we would have missed the train.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指真正的飛翔，而是指眼神快速、輕飄地移動；用來表現她緊張地在 Winston 和孩子之間掃視。
generatedExample: Her gaze flitted from the window to the door whenever she heard a sound.

explanationZhTw: 指家裡供日常活動、會客的起居室。
generatedExample: We ate dinner in the living-room because the kitchen was being painted.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 creases 指臉上的皺摺、皺紋，不是衣服上的折痕。
generatedExample: The doctor pointed out the fine creases around his eyes.

explanationZhTw: 「noisy」是吵鬧的、很大聲的意思，這裡形容孩子們吵個不停。
generatedExample: The classroom was noisy after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 「disappointed」是失望的，表示因為沒辦法去做想做的事而感到不高興。
generatedExample: She was disappointed when the concert was canceled.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「hanging」不是一般的「懸掛」，而是指「絞刑／吊死」這種處決方式，是依上下文才看得出來的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The novel describes a public hanging in the town square.

explanationZhTw: 「be back」是 phrasal verb，意思是回來、返回。
generatedExample: I will be back in ten minutes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hanging` 不是一般的「懸掛」，而是指「絞刑／上絞刑架處決」。屬於依上下文才看得出的特殊意思。
generatedExample: The villagers gathered to witness the hanging.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是指動物的「吼叫」，而是指「大聲吼道、厲聲說」。這是根據語境產生的用法。
generatedExample: She roared at the children to keep quiet.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hanging` 不是一般的「懸掛」，而是指「絞刑、上吊處決」。在小說語境中，它帶有明確的暴力與死刑意味。
generatedExample: The crowd gathered to witness the hanging at dawn.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hanging` 不是一般的「懸掛」，而是指「絞刑、上吊處決」。在小說語境中，它帶有明確的暴力與死刑意味。
generatedExample: The crowd gathered to witness the hanging at dawn.

explanationZhTw: `chanted` 指反覆用近似吟唱的方式喊叫，通常比一般的 `said` 更有節奏感，也可能帶點單調或機械式重複的語氣。
generatedExample: The fans chanted the team’s name all through the match.

explanationZhTw: `capering round` 表示「蹦蹦跳跳地亂跑、嬉鬧著轉來轉去」。這裡描寫小女孩活潑、甚至有點興奮失控的動作。
generatedExample: The children were capering round the garden after lunch.

explanationZhTw: 國際法用語，指戰爭中違反戰爭法的罪行。
generatedExample: The tribunal charged the officer with war crimes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「表演」，而是指供人圍觀、帶有展示性的場面或景象。
generatedExample: The parade was a spectacle that drew huge crowds.

explanationZhTw: 表示「大聲吵著要求」，語氣比一般的 ask 強烈很多。
generatedExample: The children clamoured for another story.

explanationZhTw: 較正式的說法，表示「向某人告辭、離開」。
generatedExample: After the meeting, I took my leave and headed home.

explanationZhTw: 表示「朝……去、往……走」，重點在移動方向。
generatedExample: She made for the exit when she heard the alarm.

explanationZhTw: 表示「突然轉身、轉過身來」。
generatedExample: He spun round when someone called his name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「把東西偷偷塞進口袋裡」，有偷偷摸摸的語感。
generatedExample: He pocketed the receipt without looking at it.

explanationZhTw: 一種可把石子或彈丸射出去的裝置，中文常譯「彈弓」或「投石器」。
generatedExample: The kids used a catapult to launch stones into the air.

explanationZhTw: 大聲吼叫、低沉地大喊。這裡表示男孩用非常大的聲音喊出來。
generatedExample: He bellowed across the room for everyone to hear.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 struck 不是「打」，而是「讓人印象深刻；使人感到／注意到」。
generatedExample: The silence struck me as unusual.

explanationZhTw: 「無助的驚恐」；helpless 表示無法自保或無法做什麼，fright 表示強烈的恐懼。
generatedExample: She looked up with helpless fright when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: 帶點灰色的、偏灰的。-ish 常表示「有點像、略帶」某種顏色或特徵。
generatedExample: His hair had turned greyish after years of stress.

explanationZhTw: `flat` 在英式英文裡是「公寓、套房」的意思，不是「平的」。這裡是依上下文才看得出來的常見用法。
generatedExample: I left my keys in the flat and had to go back.

explanationZhTw: `sat down` 是常見片語動詞，意思是「坐下」。
generatedExample: She sat down next to me and opened her notebook.

explanationZhTw: `telescreen` 是作品中的特殊名詞，指帶有監視功能的螢幕裝置。它不是一般日常英文的固定詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: `reading out` 是片語動詞，意思是「朗讀、宣讀」。這裡不是單純「讀」，而是把內容大聲說出來。
generatedExample: The teacher was reading out the names of the winners.

explanationZhTw: `with a sort of brutal relish` 表示帶著一種殘酷而得意的興奮感。這裡的 `relish` 不是「調味醬」，而是「享受、津津有味」的意思。
generatedExample: He described the accident with a brutal relish that made everyone uncomfortable.

explanationZhTw: `anchored` 原本是「下錨停泊」；這裡用在 Floating Fortress 上，表示它被固定在某處，不是一般船隻語境。
generatedExample: The platform was anchored offshore for the summer season.

explanationZhTw: 「過著恐懼不安的生活」；這裡不是字面上的「生命」，而是指整天活在害怕中。
generatedExample: After the threats, she had to lead a life of terror for months.

explanationZhTw: 「日夜不間斷地」；表示一直、持續地做某事。
generatedExample: The nurses watched the patient night and day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「symptoms」不是醫學上的「症狀」，而是「跡象、徵兆」；「unorthodoxy」指「不符合正統／主流觀念」。
generatedExample: The small changes were seen as symptoms of unorthodoxy.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb「turn into」；在這裡是「把……變成……」，帶有被動的轉變意味。
generatedExample: The quiet room turned into a noisy classroom in minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「恰恰相反」；用來強調前一句的說法不對，真正情況正好相反。
generatedExample: I thought he was angry; on the contrary, he was very patient.

explanationZhTw: 「偷聽、竊聽」；指暗中聽別人的談話，通常帶有偷偷摸摸的感覺。
generatedExample: He was eavesdropping outside the door.

explanationZhTw: 「檢舉、告發、公開指控」；這裡是把父母報告給當局。
generatedExample: She denounced the plan as unfair and dangerous.

explanationZhTw: `wear off` 是片語動詞，表示「逐漸消失、減弱」。這裡指子彈造成的刺痛感已經慢慢退去。
generatedExample: The pain from the cut had worn off by the next morning.

explanationZhTw: `pick up` 在這裡不是「撿起來」而已，而是指「拿起並重新開始做某事」。句中表示他把筆拿起來，準備勉強繼續寫。
generatedExample: She picked up her notebook and continued the lesson.

explanationZhTw: `half-heartedly` 表示「心不在焉地、沒有熱情地、敷衍地」。這裡強調他寫日記時沒有真正投入。
generatedExample: He smiled half-heartedly when he heard the joke.

explanationZhTw: 形容「完全漆黑、伸手不見五指」；這裡強調夢裡的房間黑到幾乎看不見任何東西。
generatedExample: The basement was pitch-dark after the power went out.

explanationZhTw: 表示「逐漸地、慢慢地」；不是一下子理解，而是過了一段時間才慢慢有感覺。
generatedExample: By degrees, she began to trust the new team.

explanationZhTw: `take on` 在這裡是「開始具有」的意思，整體表示「開始變得有意義／重要」。
generatedExample: The warning took on significance after the accident.

explanationZhTw: 表示「無論如何／不管怎樣」；用來把話題先收回到一個確定的結論。
generatedExample: We missed the bus, but at any rate we arrived before the meeting started.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「從黑暗中」；在這裡帶有神祕、看不清來源的感覺，表示聲音從黑暗處傳來。
generatedExample: A voice called out of the dark and made him stop.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「眼睛一閃」，而是指「快速的一瞥、短暫的眼神接觸」；在這段裡帶有微妙的暗示意味。
generatedExample: She caught a flash of the eyes from him before he looked away.

explanationZhTw: 指他們之間有種默契或心領神會的連結，不是字面上的「連結」而已。
generatedExample: Even without many words, they felt a link of understanding.

explanationZhTw: 指「偏袒某一方、黨派立場」；在這裡是說這種理解比感情或政治立場更重要。
generatedExample: The report criticized the judge for open partisanship.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「成真、實現」。
generatedExample: Her dream of writing a novel finally came true.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》裡的專有名詞，指兼具電視與監視功能的裝置；這裡不是一般日常詞，而是小說世界觀中的關鍵名詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen never stopped watching the room.

explanationZhTw: 指「小號吹出的號聲／呼號」。這裡是名詞片語，描述一陣清亮的樂聲。
generatedExample: A trumpet call announced the start of the ceremony.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指「漂浮在水上」，而是聲音「輕輕地傳來、飄進空氣中」的比喻用法。
generatedExample: Her voice floated through the open window.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡形容空氣「悶滯、不流動」，帶有死氣沉沉的感覺。
generatedExample: The room felt hot and stagnant after the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 指聲音「刺耳地、沙啞地」說出來，帶有粗糙摩擦感。
generatedExample: He answered raspingly after shouting for hours.

explanationZhTw: 「newsflash」指突發的新聞快訊、插播新聞。這裡是戰時廣播語氣，表示剛收到的最新戰況消息。
generatedExample: The newsflash interrupted the program with urgent updates from the city center.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「front」不是「前面」，而是「戰線、前線」的意思，屬於軍事語境中的特殊用法。
generatedExample: Reports from the eastern front suggested the fighting was intensifying.

explanationZhTw: 「forces」指軍隊、武裝部隊。這是新聞或軍事報導中常見的用法。
generatedExample: The country's forces were deployed to protect the border.

explanationZhTw: 「authorized」表示「被授權、被准許」。這裡指說話者有正式許可可以公開這則消息。
generatedExample: She is authorized to release the statement on behalf of the company.

explanationZhTw: 「may well」表示「很可能、完全有可能」，語氣比單純的 may 更強。
generatedExample: If we leave now, we may well catch the last train.

explanationZhTw: 這個說法是修辭性的，意思是「非常接近、幾乎到了」。不是字面上真的有可測量的距離。
generatedExample: After months of work, the team was within measurable distance of finishing the project.

explanationZhTw: 此處的「following on」表示「緊接著、接著出現」，說明後面的消息是在前一個事件之後立刻跟上來的。
generatedExample: Following on the meeting, we sent the report to everyone.

explanationZhTw: 「annihilation」在這裡是「徹底消滅、全毀」的意思，語氣比一般的 destroy 更強，常用於戰爭或暴力語境。
generatedExample: The explosion caused the annihilation of the old bridge.

explanationZhTw: 「stupendous」在這裡不是單純「很大」，而是「大得驚人、誇張地巨大」，帶有強烈驚訝語氣。
generatedExample: They reported stupendous sales after the product launch.

explanationZhTw: 「ration」在這裡是「配給量、定額供應的份量」，特別指被限制發放的食物份額。
generatedExample: During the shortage, each family received a small ration of rice.

explanationZhTw: 「belch」指打嗝或嗝出氣體；這裡是過去式，表示溫斯頓又打了一個嗝。
generatedExample: He belched loudly after finishing the soda.

explanationZhTw: 「wear off」是片語動詞，表示效果逐漸消退。這裡指杜松子酒的酒勁慢慢退去。
generatedExample: The painkillers wore off after a few hours.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「deflated」不是字面上的「漏氣」，而是形容人感到沒精神、空虛或被打擊。
generatedExample: She looked deflated after hearing the news.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是小說中的專有名詞，指一種可傳輸影像並監視人的裝置；在這裡是帶有反烏托邦色彩的科技詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room never stopped watching them.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，意思是「立正、站得筆直」，常見於軍事或正式場合。
generatedExample: The recruits stood to attention when the officer entered.

explanationZhTw: 「gave way to」在這裡表示「被……取代、轉成」；原本的音樂停下來，換成較輕快的音樂。
generatedExample: The rainy afternoon gave way to a bright evening.

explanationZhTw: 「walked over to」表示「走到……那裡」；是用走路移動到某個位置。
generatedExample: She walked over to the desk to sign the form.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「刻意讓背朝向……」；也就是他故意背對著那個裝置。
generatedExample: He spoke quietly, keeping his back to the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是小說中的專有名詞，指一種結合螢幕與監控功能的裝置，不是一般日常的電視。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the hall never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: 「reverberating」在這裡是指聲音「迴盪、回響」；形容爆炸聲持續在空中震盪傳開。
generatedExample: A reverberating bang shook the windows.

explanationZhTw: 「were falling on」在這裡不是單純的「掉落」，而是指炸彈「落到、擊中」倫敦。
generatedExample: Missiles were falling on the city after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「被風吹得拍打、飄動」；不是一般的「拍打」動作，而是風把海報吹得上下翻動。
generatedExample: The curtains flapped in the open window all night.

explanationZhTw: 表示「來回、前後擺動」；是固定說法。
generatedExample: The boat rocked to and fro in the waves.

explanationZhTw: 表示「斷斷續續地、不穩定地」；這裡指字樣在風中時隱時現。
generatedExample: She slept fitfully because of the noise outside.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指黨的意識形態「英社」；屬於虛構政治術語。
generatedExample: Ingsoc is the ruling ideology in the novel.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的專有名詞，指黨的意識形態「英社」；屬於虛構政治術語。
generatedExample: Ingsoc is the ruling ideology in the novel.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的術語，指被設計來限制思想的官方語言。
generatedExample: Newspeak was meant to narrow the range of thought.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的術語，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的想法。
generatedExample: Doublethink lets people believe two opposite claims at once.

explanationZhTw: 表示「可變性、容易被改變」；這裡特指過去會被改寫。
generatedExample: The mutability of public opinion surprised the campaign team.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「漫無目的地走、飄蕩」；帶有迷失感。
generatedExample: After the meeting, he spent an hour wandering around the city.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，不是字面上的森林；用來形容海底像茂密、陌生又令人不安的地方。
generatedExample: The coral looked like a forest on the sea bottom.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純指「巨大的」，而是指「可怕的、駭人的、非正常的」。
generatedExample: The storm created a monstrous scene across the coast.

explanationZhTw: 表示「人、活生生的人」；這種說法帶有疏離感，像把人當成一種生物。
generatedExample: No human creature can work without rest forever.

explanationZhTw: 表示「站在他這邊、支持他」；是固定用法。
generatedExample: She knew her friend was on her side during the debate.

explanationZhTw: 表示「統治、支配、控制」；這裡指政黨對社會的掌控。
generatedExample: The empire expanded its dominion over the region.

explanationZhTw: 表示「持續存在、維持下去」；這裡指黨的統治是否會一直延續。
generatedExample: The bridge was built to endure harsh winters.

explanationZhTw: 表示「標語、口號」；通常是簡短、重複、用來宣傳理念的句子。
generatedExample: The campaign used simple slogans to win support.

explanationZhTw: 這是反烏托邦口號式的反諷表達，字面上「戰爭」和「和平」相反，但在這裡表示政權用永遠的戰爭來維持內部秩序，讓人民相信和平反而來自戰爭。
generatedExample: In the novel, 'War is peace' is a slogan that shows how the regime twists language to control people.

explanationZhTw: 這句話是刻意顛倒概念的口號，意思不是「自由真的等於奴隸」，而是在這個政權的語境中，個人若追求絕對自由，反而會失去安全與獨立，最後被體制操控。
generatedExample: The slogan 'Freedom is slavery' makes the opposite of freedom sound dangerous and manipulative.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是諷刺性的政治標語，表示人民越不了解真相，就越容易被統治者操控，因此「無知」被包裝成一種對政權有利的「力量」。
generatedExample: 'Ignorance is strength' suggests that people stay easier to control when they do not know the truth.

explanationZhTw: 標語、口號；用來簡短宣傳政治理念或品牌訊息的句子。
generatedExample: The campaign used simple slogans to reach more voters.

explanationZhTw: 刻上；把字或圖案永久寫或刻在表面上。
generatedExample: The plaque was inscribed with the names of the donors.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的「老大哥」；在這裡指掌控一切、無所不在監控人民的極權象徵，不是字面上的哥哥。
generatedExample: In the novel, Big Brother represents constant state surveillance.

explanationZhTw: 追著你不放；這裡帶有被監視、揮之不去的感覺。
generatedExample: The feeling of guilt pursued him for years.

explanationZhTw: 包圍、籠罩；像整個把人罩住。
generatedExample: A thick fog was enveloping the city by morning.

explanationZhTw: 在戶外；較正式或老派的說法。
generatedExample: We spent most of the summer out of doors.

explanationZhTw: 立方公分；體積單位。這裡用來強調只剩下非常小的一點點私密空間。
generatedExample: The bottle holds 250 cubic centimetres of liquid.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「shifted round」是指位置或方向改變了，不是很正式的說法；可理解為「轉到另一邊、移動了角度」。
generatedExample: By noon, the table had shifted round so the chairs were facing the window.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「grim」不是單純「嚴肅」，而是「陰森的、令人不安的、冷峻的」氣氛。
generatedExample: The abandoned hospital looked grim in the rain.

explanationZhTw: 「quailed before」表示「在……面前感到害怕、退縮」。這裡強調主角面對龐大建築時的畏懼感。
generatedExample: She quailed before the crowd and could not speak.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「stormed」是「以武力強攻、硬闖奪取」的意思，不是天氣的暴風。
generatedExample: The rebels stormed the palace at dawn.

explanationZhTw: 「batter down」是指「猛烈敲擊到倒下、擊毀」。這裡表示再多武力也攻不破。
generatedExample: They tried to batter the door down, but it held.

explanationZhTw: 「annihilation」表示「徹底毀滅、完全消滅」，程度比一般的 destroy 更強。
generatedExample: The explosion left the village in total annihilation.

explanationZhTw: 「reduced to ashes」是慣用說法，表示「被燒成灰燼、徹底毀掉」。
generatedExample: After the fire, the letters were reduced to ashes.

explanationZhTw: 「wipe out」是「徹底抹除、消滅」；整個片語表示「讓它從世界上完全消失」。
generatedExample: A single mistake should not wipe your efforts out of existence.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「trace」是名詞，意思是「痕跡、蛛絲馬跡、任何殘留的證據」。
generatedExample: There was no trace of the missing key anywhere.

explanationZhTw: 「anonymous」表示「匿名的、無名的」，也就是不知道是誰寫的。
generatedExample: An anonymous email warned us about the problem.

explanationZhTw: 「scribbled」表示「潦草地寫下」，通常帶有匆忙、字跡不工整的感覺。
generatedExample: He scribbled the phone number on a receipt.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的專有名詞，指一種兼具廣播與監視功能的裝置；在此是小說世界觀裡的重要科技名詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the hotel room never seemed to switch off.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 struck 不是「打擊」，而是指時鐘敲響十四下，也就是 14:00；屬於依語境理解的用法。
generatedExample: At noon, the old clock struck twelve in the tower.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「讓他重新有了勇氣、精神或希望」。不是字面上的「把新心放進去」。
generatedExample: Her encouragement put new heart into the team before the final match.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 ghost 是比喻性的用法，形容這個人像幽靈一樣孤單、沒有存在感，也帶有失去生命力的感覺。
generatedExample: After weeks of working alone, he felt like a lonely ghost in the office.

explanationZhTw: utter 在這裡不是「發出聲音」的常見用法，而是較正式地表示「說出、講出」。
generatedExample: She was uttering a warning when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: continuity 指「連續性、不中斷的狀態」。這裡的 broken 也是比喻用法，表示這種連續感沒有被切斷。
generatedExample: One missed meeting should not mean the continuity of the project is broken.

explanationZhTw: sane 在這裡是「保持理智、精神正常」；整個片語強調在壓力下仍維持清醒和穩定。
generatedExample: He survived the stressful year by staying sane and keeping a routine.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「繼續下去、維持下去」。
generatedExample: Even after the setback, they carried on with the experiment.

explanationZhTw: heritage 是「遺產、傳承、文化資產」。這裡指人類文明與精神價值的延續。
generatedExample: Libraries help preserve our human heritage for future generations.

explanationZhTw: 「future」是 A2 常見字，意思是「未來」。這裡和「past」對照，指向一個還未到來的時間。
generatedExample: They hope the future will be better than today.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「undone」不是一般的「未完成」，而是指「無法撤回、無法恢復原狀」。整句強調一旦做了就不能再改回去。
generatedExample: Once the file is deleted, the damage cannot be undone.

explanationZhTw: 「uniformity」指「一致性、同質化」。在這裡是帶有政治意味的抽象名詞，表示一切都被壓成同一種樣子。
generatedExample: The school values uniformity in its rules and behavior.

explanationZhTw: 「solitude」是「獨處、孤寂」；和一般的「alone」不同，語氣更正式，也更偏向孤單的狀態。
generatedExample: He enjoyed the solitude of the mountain cabin.

explanationZhTw: 「Big Brother」在《1984》裡不是字面上的「大哥哥」，而是象徵無所不在的監控權力與極權政權。
generatedExample: People in the novel fear that Big Brother is always watching.

explanationZhTw: 「doublethink」是作品中的關鍵術語，指同時接受兩個互相矛盾的想法，還相信兩者都是真的。
generatedExample: Doublethink lets the regime control what people believe.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「greetings」是正式的「問候、致意」，放在句尾帶有宣告、呼喊的語氣，不只是普通的打招呼。
generatedExample: He raised his hand in greetings to the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是「反射」的意思，而是「他心想／他暗自想著」，表示人物在腦中進行內心思考。
generatedExample: She reflected that she should have left earlier.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「清楚地表達、組織成語句」。這裡指他開始能把自己的想法整理成可說出的形式。
generatedExample: He needed a minute to formulate his answer.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「關鍵的一步、決定性的一步」，表示這一步對後續結果有重大影響。
generatedExample: Applying for the job felt like a decisive step in her career.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「後果、影響」。這裡是在說每個行為本身就包含了它會帶來的結果。
generatedExample: The consequences of that choice were serious.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「導致、使成為必要結果」；在這裡是說思想罪不只是「會導致」死亡，而是被明確定義為與死亡等同。
generatedExample: The new rule may entail extra paperwork for everyone.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》中的造詞，指「思想罪」：心中出現與政權相違的想法本身就被視為罪。
generatedExample: In the story, even silent disagreement can count as thoughtcrime.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「就是死亡」，而是強烈的隱喻，表示「等同於死亡、必死無疑」。
generatedExample: In that regime, a single mistake is death.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「死人」，而是指「已經暴露、幾乎注定會出事的人」；帶有「死定了」的意思。
generatedExample: After the leak, he felt like a dead man at work.

explanationZhTw: 指「沾了墨水的」；這裡是在描述手指上有墨漬。
generatedExample: Her inkstained notes were hard to read.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思是「暴露你、洩露你的身分或行蹤」，不是「背叛你」。
generatedExample: A small mistake can betray you to the police.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 nosing 不是「鼻子」，而是形容「愛探聽、愛偷看別人隱私的」；有窺探的意思。
generatedExample: A nosing neighbor kept asking about their visitors.

explanationZhTw: 英式用法，指「午餐休息時間」或「午休時段」。
generatedExample: We talked during the lunch interval.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「暗示一下」或「放出一點線索」，不直接明說。
generatedExample: She dropped a hint that she wanted flowers.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，意思是「用力擦掉」；這裡是把墨水徹底洗掉。
generatedExample: He scrubbed the mud away with a brush.

explanationZhTw: rasp 原本可指「刮擦出刺耳聲」，這裡是形容肥皂摩擦皮膚時很粗糙、很刮皮膚的感覺。
generatedExample: The rough fabric rasped my skin.

explanationZhTw: 這是較正式的說法，意思是「很適合做這件事／很能勝任這個用途」。
generatedExample: This tool is well adapted for this purpose.

explanationZhTw: 把日記收起來；這裡的 `put away` 表示「收好、放回原處」，不是單純「放下」。
generatedExample: She put the keys away after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 沒用的；在這裡表示「做這件事沒有意義或效果」，語氣帶有判斷性。
generatedExample: This old map is useless now.

explanationZhTw: 確認；確保。這裡是先檢查事情是否真的發生，而不是「讓」某件事發生。
generatedExample: Please make sure the door is locked.

explanationZhTw: 存在；這裡指日記「存在這件事」，語意偏抽象，不是指某個人活著。
generatedExample: They kept its existence secret for years.

explanationZhTw: 可辨認的；能被看出或識別出來的。這裡強調那一粒灰塵夠明顯，能被人注意到。
generatedExample: The logo was still identifiable after the rain.

explanationZhTw: 放置；擺放。這裡不是銀行用的「存入」，而是較正式地表示把東西小心放到某處。
generatedExample: He deposited the letter on the desk.

explanationZhTw: 注定會；很可能會。這是一個固定說法，表示結果幾乎不可避免。
generatedExample: If you rush, you're bound to make mistakes.

explanationZhTw: 被甩掉；被抖落。這裡是 `shake off` 的被動形式，表示東西因移動而脫落。
generatedExample: The snow was shaken off his coat.
